channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Illinois governor blocks pardoned Jan. 6 rioters from state jobs
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker (D) said he would block pardoned Capitol rioters from serving in local government positions, citing a state law ordinance, according to a Thursday letter obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump to meet with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is meeting Friday with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, whose company designs and supplies the advanced computer chips that play an integral role in developing artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Kevin Freking And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon strips travel reimbursement for troops seeking abortions, fertility treatment
item.description: A new memo says the Defense Department will no longer reimburse service members for travel out of state to get reproductive health care, including abortions and fertility treatments.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House says Trump tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China will come Saturday. No word on exemptions
item.description: Canada and Mexico say they’ve prepared the option of retaliatory tariffs to be used if necessary now that the White House says the U.S. will put in place 25% tariffs on imports from them this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats rally around lightning rod issue during unruly DNC debate despite voter backlash in 2024
item.description: There was a heavy focus on systemic racism and diversity, equity and inclusion programs during the final debate among the eight candidates vying to chair the Democratic National Committee, as the party aims to exit the political wilderness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to cut the federal workforce. Who they are and what that means
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to cut the federal workforce by 10 percent might not save as much money as hoped and could have unintended consequences. NPR explains why.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Top 25: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick unveils priority bills for Texas Senate
item.description: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick has announced the first round of his top 40 priority bills for the 89th Texas legislative session, outlining a conservative agenda that includes school choice, banning THC, and placing the Ten Commandments in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP senator faces intraparty criticism after saying he is 'struggling' with RFK Jr's confirmation
item.description: The top Republican on the Senate's health committee, Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., faced criticism from fellow Republicans after he suggested his vote for Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s confirmation as Health and Human Services secretary is not a lock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal court rules ATF age limits on handgun sales violate Second Amendment
item.description: A three-judge panel of the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in New Orleans found a federal law that prohibits adults aged 18-20 from buying handguns violates the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s FCC chief opens investigation into NPR and PBS
item.description: Brendan Carr, the new head of the Federal Communications Commission under President Trump, says the public broadcasters are being investigated for allegedly running commercials.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Senator calls RFK Jr.’s position on race and vaccines dangerous
item.description: In one of the most tense exchanges in a heated confirmation hearing, Senator Angela Alsobrooks called out past comments RFK Jr. made suggesting a different vaccine schedule for Black people.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Weekend vote to decide if Ken Martin jumps from Minnesota Democratic perch to national party head
item.description: Members of the Democratic National Committee are due to vote Saturday on who will lead the party. After the 2024 election, Democrats are regrouping.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: US aid agency is in upheaval during foreign assistance freeze and staff departures
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump administration changes have upended the U.S. agency charged with providing humanitarian aid to countries overseas, with dozens of senior officials put on leave, thousands of contractors laid off, and a sweeping freeze imposed on billions of dollars in foreign assistance.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers clash over immigration policies, in-state tuition for Dreamers
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — As Gov. Ron DeSantis and legislative leaders in Tallahassee clash on immigration proposals, DeSantis said he's planning to veto the recently passed TRUMP Act. That move could put lawmakers back to square one.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump VA pick Doug Collins advances to full Senate vote
item.description: Doug Collins, President Donald Trump's nominee for VA chief, passed a Senate cloture vote Thursday night and will proceed to a full vote on the Senate floor for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Why some Minnesota law enforcement agencies will not follow Trump’s mass deportation plan
item.description: Besides laws which prevent local police from enforcing immigration law, some officials say they don’t have the resources to take on additional tasks.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Black Caucus blasts Trump's DEI comments after Potomac plane crash
item.description: "Not only are the President’s claims untrue, they also speak to the Republican Party’s desire to divide us as a country.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Senators grill 3 of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks in confirmation hearings
item.description: Three of President Donald Trump’s cabinet picks faced skepticism and intense grilling from Democratic senators during their confirmation hearings on Thursday. What we’re following: 1. Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly D.C. plane crash begins
item.description: As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's pulling a U-turn on EVs, but not much has changed
item.description: President Donald Trump has charted a new course for electric vehicle policy in the U.S. While the direction is clear, the details aren't.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske, MPR News

item.title: Trump order cracks down on antisemitism and could deport foreign student protesters
item.description: The executive order directs government agencies to use all available tools to prosecute or remove perpetrators of antisemitic harassment and violence, especially on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Frey formally launches bid for third term as Minneapolis mayor
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said Wednesday he’ll seek a third — and final — term as mayor to continue his administration’s work overhauling the police department and building more affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling the security protections and clearance of retired Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Mark Milley.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida legislative leaders respond to DeSantis attacks, imply he didn't read TRUMP Act or is lying
item.description: Florida's congressional leaders fired back at Gov. Ron DeSantis after he accused of them of "theatrics" Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
